dry_bulk.confluence_note: A stark contrast exists between the BDI hitting the 99.5th percentile with a Z-score of +2.16 and the 'negative' fundamental rating driven by weakening Chinese steel production and high iron ore inventories.
dry_bulk.summary: Spot rates for Capesize and Supramax vessels remain in a technical expansion regime, yet volatility has collapsed to multi-year lows. Analysts warn of a significant second-half slowdown in Asia as China's iron ore portside inventories sit 21.9% higher year-over-year while steel production contracts.
dry_bulk.outlook: Bearish for the second half of 2026 as macro risks and industrial slowdowns begin to weigh on record-high spot rates.
dry_bulk.watch: China iron ore inventory levels and potential further contractions in coal import demand.

tanker.confluence_note: Extremely high Z-scores (+2.95 for Clean) align with 'positive' fundamental ratings and structural supply disruptions caused by unnavigable straits.
tanker.summary: The tanker market is sustaining an aggressive expansion regime with Clean Tanker ROC60 at a staggering +143.9%. Geopolitical tensions and longer ton-miles are offsetting negative momentum signals, as oil deliveries dry out and inventories continue to draw. Segment breadth remains important: clean tankers at 2178.0 (ROC60 +143.9%), alongside dirty tankers at 2811.0 (ROC60 +70.0%).
key_signals: Middle East-Asia VLCC rates up 71.1% YOY
tanker.outlook: Strongly bullish as supply-side constraints and geopolitical risk premiums dominate the freight environment.
tanker.watch: Navigability of major trade straits and Middle East conflict escalation/de-escalation.

macro_note: A widening divide is emerging between energy and industrial shipping; geopolitical disruptions are fueling a tanker super-cycle while Chinese industrial fatigue threatens the sustainability of the dry bulk rally.
